channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: What's the key to surviving a plane crash? Skilled crew, strong seats and wings that detach
item.description: After watching videos of a Delta Air Lines jet catch fire upon landing and flip over on a Toronto runway, it's fair to wonder how anyone could have survived.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Labor unions call on Trump to boost US shipbuilding against increasing Chinese dominance
item.description: The heads of four major labor unions are calling on President Donald Trump to boost American shipbuilding and enforce tariffs and other “strong penalties” against China for its increasing dominance.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pakistan steps up arrests of Afghans without papers. Kabul says it's a move to expel all refugees
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Authorities have stepped up arrests of Afghan citizens in Pakistan's capital and a nearby city in an effort that the Afghan Embassy in Islamabad described on Wednesday as a push to force the expulsion of all Afghan refugees from the country.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A look at the 8 Latin American crime groups designated as terrorist organizations by the US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The United States government is formally designating eight Latin American organized crime groups that also operate in the U.S. to be “foreign terrorist organizations.” They are involved in drug trafficking, migrant smuggling and extend their territory through violence.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil’s Bolsonaro slams 'vague' accusations after attempted coup indictment
item.description: Brazil’s former President Jair Bolsonaro on Wednesday denied accusations that he orchestrated a coup to stay in office and said the charges were the result of an authoritarian regime manufacturing “internal enemies.”.
item.dc:creator: Eléonore Hughes, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump to meet with Macron, Starmer next week
item.description: President Trump is set to meet next week with two key European leaders amid tensions over Trump's approach to negotiating an end to the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Boris Johnson: Trump's Ukraine comments meant to 'shock' Europe into action
item.description: Former U.K. Prime Minister Boris Johnson said President Trump’s recent remarks about Ukraine are intended to “shock” Europe into action.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: US condemns 'dangerous' Chinese maneuvers after near miss with Philippine patrol plane
item.description: The U.S. condemned what it called “dangerous” Chinese maneuvers over the South China Sea after a close encounter between a Chinese helicopter and a Philippine patrol plane on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Italy PM Meloni visits pope in hospital, says he's in good spirits as tests show slight improvement
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis for 20 minutes in the hospital Wednesday and reported he was “alert and responsive” and full of good humor, despite his diagnosis of pneumonia and a complicated bronchial infection that has sidelined the 88-year-old pontiff for six days
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, ANDREA ROSA and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Italian Prime Minister Meloni visits Pope Francis in hospital, says he's in good spirits
item.description: Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis in hospital on Wednesday and reported he was full of good humor following his diagnosis of pneumonia and a bronchial infection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Trump can stop Iran's march to nuclear weapons: 're-establish credible military threat', report says
item.description: A new report from the Foundation for the Defense of Democracies provides recommendations for how the Trump administration can stop Tehran's march to produce a nuclear bomb.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico president says her government requested US surveillance drone flights
item.description: Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum says surveillance drone flights by the U.S. government over Mexico are occurring in collaboration with and at the request of her government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's foreign aid freeze halted a crucial program fighting HIV in Africa. Here's what's at stake
item.description: The freeze on U.S. foreign aid ordered by President Donald Trump has effectively halted one of the world's most successful responses to a disease.
item.dc:creator: Farai Mutsaka And Gerald Imray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A worried NATO holds large-scale combat drills as the US stance on Europe shifts under Trump
item.description: SMARDAN, Romania (AP) — NATO members continued their largest combat exercises of 2025 on Wednesday, testing their ability to rapidly deploy large-scale forces on the 32-nation alliance’s eastern border as worries grow over its most powerful member, the United States.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and NICOLAE DUMITRACHE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hawley calls for new inspector general to investigate Ukraine spending
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) pledged to introduce legislation to track U.S. dollars designated for Ukraine in its war against Russia, after citing the possibility of corrupt spending under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Iranian crown prince calls for global action against Khamenei's regime
item.description: Iranian Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi demanded global action against the Islamic Republic's regime while delivering the keynote address at the Geneva Summit for Human Rights and Democracy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Experts give up hope for 157 false killer whales stranded on remote Australian beach
item.description: Marine experts have given up hope of rescuing more than 150 false killer whales that became stranded on a remote beach on Australia’s island state of Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump crafted 'win-win' situation for US, Ukraine economic plan: Bessent
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said during an interview that President Trump crafted a “win-win” situation for the potential U.S.-Ukraine economic plan, a proposal that, in his view, will boost Kyiv’s security and bring the two countries into a closer relationship.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: At least 18 killed in stampede at New Delhi railway station in India as Hindu pilgrims travel to festival
item.description: In New Delhi, India, at least 18 people were killed in a stampede at a railway station. Many victims were Hindu pilgrims traveling to the Maha Kumbh festival.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fans in Montreal loudly boo US anthem prior to Americans' 4 Nations Face-Off game vs. Canada
item.description: Fans in Montreal loudly booed the U.S. national anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off game between the Americans and Canada on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump, Modi meet at Oval Office as US weighs tariffs
item.description: Before speaking with Trump, Modi met separately with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, a Trump administration official.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: A gas explosion at a department store in Taiwan on Thursday killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Steel tariffs could disrupt international bridge expansion
item.description: The steel 2-lane Starr-Camargo International Bridge in Rio Grande City, Texas, is gearing up for an expansion of lanes, however they might not be steel after 25% tariffs were imposed by the Trump administration, the bridge's owner Sam Vale told Border Report on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9
